Name: neorv32_imem.mem_ram_b0, mem_ram_b1, mem_ram_b2, mem_ram_b3
Description: Four byte-wide RAM slices that together implement the 32-bit instruction memory when IMEM is configured as RAM. Each slice stores one byte of the instruction word. Writes are selective via bus_req_i.ben, and reads assemble the four slices into rdata.
Justification: These slices are the authoritative program store; any unintended write-enable, bit flip, or byte-lane corruption alters opcodes/immediates and can redirect control flow or execute unintended code, so code-store integrity is critical.

Name: neorv32_imem.rdata
Description: A 32-bit register that captures and outputs instruction words from IMEM (ROM or RAM). It updates on clock edges and is driven on bus responses when reads are active.
Justification: rdata is the instruction presented to fetch/decode; corruption, stale sampling, or byte-lane mix-ups yield the wrong opcode to the CPU, causing incorrect execution, so its integrity must be preserved.

Name: neorv32_imem.addr
Description: Word-aligned address derived from bus_req_i.addr(addr_hi_c downto 2). Used to index instruction memory for both reads and writes.
Justification: addr selects which instruction word is fetched or written; if manipulated or computed incorrectly, the core fetches from the wrong location, enabling code redirection or silent corruption.

Name: neorv32_imem.rden
Description: A single-bit registered signal derived from bus_req_i.stb and not bus_req_i.rw. It gates whether rdata is driven onto bus_rsp_o.data.
Justification: rden governs when instruction data is driven; if stuck low, instruction fetches never complete and the pipeline stalls, while spurious assertion can cause fetch glitches—both degrading forward progress.
